Let me outline the code:

#include <queue>
#include <map>
#include <tuple>
using namespace std;

struct Fraction {
    int num;
    int den;
    Fraction(int n, int d) : num(n), den(d) {
        // Normalize the fraction
        int g = gcd(abs(n), abs(d));
        num = n / g;
        den = d / g;
        if (den < 0) {
            num = -num;
            den = -den;
        }
    }
};

namespace std {
    template<> struct hash<Fraction> {
        size_t operator()(const Fraction& f) const {
            return hash<int>()(f.num) ^ (hash<int>()(f.den) << 1);
        }
    };
}

But wait, this is not standard C++, as we can't specialize std::hash for a user-defined type in the std namespace. So, perhaps it's better to use a map instead of an unordered_map.

std::map<std::pair<int, int>, int> visited;

But in C++, the pair is ordered lex, so it can be used as a key in a map.

So, for each new fraction, we create a pair (num, den) and check if it's in the map.

Now, putting it all together.

The code would look something like this:

class Solution {
public:
    int leastOpsExpressTarget(int x, int target) {
        // Handle the case where target is 0, but according to the problem statement, target is positive.
        if (target == x) return 0; // No operations needed.

        queue<tuple<int, int, int>> q;
        map<pair<int, int>, int> visited;

        // Initial state: x / 1, 0 operations.
        q.push(make_tuple(x, 1, 0));
        visited[{x, 1}] = 0;

        while (!q.empty()) {
            auto current = q.front();
            q.pop();
            int num = get<0>(current);
            int den = get<1>(current);
            int ops = get<2>(current);

            // Check if current value is the target.
            if (num == target * den) { // Because num/den == target
                return ops;
            }

            // Apply all four operations.
            // 1. Addition: current + x → (num + x*den)/den
            int new_num_add = num + x * den;
            int new_den_add = den;
            int g = gcd(abs(new_num_add), abs(new_den_add));
            new_num_add /= g;
            new_den_add /= g;
            if (new_den_add < 0) {
                new_num_add = -new_num_add;
                new_den_add = -new_den_add;
            }
            if (visited.find({new_num_add, new_den_add}) == visited.end() || ops + 1 < visited[{new_num_add, new_den_add}]) {
                visited[{new_num_add, new_den_add}] = ops + 1;
                q.push(make_tuple(new_num_add, new_den_add, ops + 1));
            }

            // 2. Subtraction: current - x → (num - x*den)/den
            int new_num_sub = num - x * den;
            int new_den_sub = den;
            g = gcd(abs(new_num_sub), abs(new_den_sub));
            new_num_sub /= g;
            new_den_sub /= g;
            if (new_den_sub < 0) {
                new_num_sub = -new_num_sub;
                new_den_sub = -new_den_sub;
            }
            if (visited.find({new_num_sub, new_den_sub}) == visited.end() || ops + 1 < visited[{new_num_sub, new_den_sub}]) {
                visited[{new_num_sub, new_den_sub}] = ops + 1;
                q.push(make_tuple(new_num_sub, new_den_sub, ops + 1));
            }

            // 3. Multiplication: current * x → (num * x)/den
            int new_num_mul = num * x;
            int new_den_mul = den;
            g = gcd(abs(new_num_mul), abs(new_den_mul));
            new_num_mul /= g;
            new_den_mul /= g;
            if (new_den_mul < 0) {
                new_num_mul = -new_num_mul;
                new_den_mul = -new_den_mul;
            }
            if (visited.find({new_num_mul, new_den_mul}) == visited.end() || ops + 1 < visited[{new_num_mul, new_den_mul}]) {
                visited[{new_num_mul, new_den_mul}] = ops + 1;
                q.push(make_tuple(new_num_mul, new_den_mul, ops + 1));
            }

            // 4. Division: current / x → num/(den * x)
            int new_num_div = num;
            int new_den_div = den * x;
            g = gcd(abs(new_num_div), abs(new_den_div));
            new_num_div /= g;
            new_den_div /= g;
            if (new_den_div < 0) {
                new_num_div = -new_num_div;
                new_den_div = -new_den_div;
            }
            if (visited.find({new_num_div, new_den_div}) == visited.end() || ops + 1 < visited[{new_num_div, new_den_div}]) {
                visited[{new_num_div, new_den_div}] = ops + 1;
                q.push(make_tuple(new_num_div, new_den_div, ops + 1));
            }
        }

        // If target not reachable (though problem says it's possible)
        return -1;
    }

    int gcd(int a, int b) {
        while (b) {
            a %= b;
            swap(a, b);
        }
        return a;
    }
};

using namespace std;

struct Fraction {
    int num;
    int den;
    Fraction(int n, int d) : num(n), den(d) {
        // Normalize the fraction
        int g = gcd(abs(n), abs(d));
        num = n / g;
        den = d / g;
        if (den < 0) {
            num = -num;
            den = -den;
        }
    }
};

namespace std {
    template<> struct hash<Fraction> {
        size_t operator()(const Fraction& f) const {
            return hash<int>()(f.num) ^ (hash<int>()(f.den) << 1);
        }
    };
}

The code would look something like this:

class Solution {
public:
    int leastOpsExpressTarget(int x, int target) {
        // Handle the case where target is 0, but according to the problem statement, target is positive.
        if (target == x) return 0; // No operations needed.

        queue<tuple<int, int, int>> q;
        map<pair<int, int>, int> visited;

        // Initial state: x / 1, 0 operations.
        q.push(make_tuple(x, 1, 0));
        visited[{x, 1}] = 0;

        while (!q.empty()) {
            auto current = q.front();
            q.pop();
            int num = get<0>(current);
            int den = get<1>(current);
            int ops = get<2>(current);

            // Check if current value is the target.
            if (num == target * den) { // Because num/den == target
                return ops;
            }

            // Apply all four operations.
            // 1. Addition: current + x → (num + x*den)/den
            int new_num_add = num + x * den;
            int new_den_add = den;
            int g = gcd(abs(new_num_add), abs(new_den_add));
            new_num_add /= g;
            new_den_add /= g;
            if (new_den_add < 0) {
                new_num_add = -new_num_add;
                new_den_add = -new_den_add;
            }
            if (visited.find({new_num_add, new_den_add}) == visited.end() || ops + 1 < visited[{new_num_add, new_den_add}]) {
                visited[{new_num_add, new_den_add}] = ops + 1;
                q.push(make_tuple(new_num_add, new_den_add, ops + 1));
            }

            // 2. Subtraction: current - x → (num - x*den)/den
            int new_num_sub = num - x * den;
            int new_den_sub = den;
            g = gcd(abs(new_num_sub), abs(new_den_sub));
            new_num_sub /= g;
            new_den_sub /= g;
            if (new_den_sub < 0) {
                new_num_sub = -new_num_sub;
                new_den_sub = -new_den_sub;
            }
            if (visited.find({new_num_sub, new_den_sub}) == visited.end() || ops + 1 < visited[{new_num_sub, new_den_sub}]) {
                visited[{new_num_sub, new_den_sub}] = ops + 1;
                q.push(make_tuple(new_num_sub, new_den_sub, ops + 1));
            }

            // 3. Multiplication: current * x → (num * x)/den
            int new_num_mul = num * x;
            int new_den_mul = den;
            g = gcd(abs(new_num_mul), abs(new_den_mul));
            new_num_mul /= g;
            new_den_mul /= g;
            if (new_den_mul < 0) {
                new_num_mul = -new_num_mul;
                new_den_mul = -new_den_mul;
            }
            if (visited.find({new_num_mul, new_den_mul}) == visited.end() || ops + 1 < visited[{new_num_mul, new_den_mul}]) {
                visited[{new_num_mul, new_den_mul}] = ops + 1;
                q.push(make_tuple(new_num_mul, new_den_mul, ops + 1));
            }

            // 4. Division: current / x → num/(den * x)
            int new_num_div = num;
            int new_den_div = den * x;
            g = gcd(abs(new_num_div), abs(new_den_div));
            new_num_div /= g;
            new_den_div /= g;
            if (new_den_div < 0) {
                new_num_div = -new_num_div;
                new_den_div = -new_den_div;
            }
            if (visited.find({new_num_div, new_den_div}) == visited.end() || ops + 1 < visited[{new_num_div, new_den_div}]) {
                visited[{new_num_div, new_den_div}] = ops + 1;
                q.push(make_tuple(new_num_div, new_den_div, ops + 1));
            }
        }

        // If target not reachable (though problem says it's possible)
        return -1;
    }

    int gcd(int a, int b) {
        while (b) {
            a %= b;
            swap(a, b);
        }
        return a;
    }
};
